revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「梶原神社（かじわらじんじゃ）は、宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町高石浜にある神社。御祭神は源頼朝公、梶原景時公、梶原景季公。鎌倉武将梶原景時の兄、梶原景実が創建。 交通アクセス JR八幡大橋駅からバスで高石浜停留所下車、徒歩5分」
revision.text:
梶原神社（かじわらじんじゃ）は、宮城県気仙沼市唐桑町高石浜にある神社。御祭神は源頼朝公、梶原景時公、梶原景季公。鎌倉武将梶原景時の兄、梶原景実が創建。
交通アクセス JR八幡大橋駅からバスで高石浜停留所下車、徒歩5分

梶原神社（かじわらじんじゃ）は、[[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜にある神社。御祭神は[[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公。鎌倉武将[[梶原景時]]の兄、梶原景実が創建。
== 交通アクセス == JR八幡大橋駅からバスで高石浜停留所下車、徒歩5分

{{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |社格 = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''梶原神社'''（かじわらじんじゃ）は、[[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜にある神社。御祭神は[[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公。鎌倉武将[[梶原景時]]の兄、梶原景実が創建。
==祭神== [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公の三柱を祀る。
==歴史== [[建保]]5年([[1217年]])、 [[鶴岡八幡宮]]の臨時[[別当]]であった[[梶原景実]]は、[[源頼朝]]公の死亡、これを追うかのような[[梶原]]一族の没落、又、[[和田氏]]、[[畠山氏]]が滅んで行くのを見て、世を憂い鎌倉を離れ、[[蝦夷]] [[千島]]を目指して下り、[[藤原高衡]] (本吉四郎高衡)ゆかりの地である[[気仙沼市]] [[唐桑町]]石浜にたどり着いた。 家の脇に社を建て、[[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公の御影を安置し、一族の冥福を祈り[[梶原神社]]を建立した。 [[梶原景実]]の僧名は[[専光房良暹]]。[[梶原景時]]の兄で、[[源頼朝]]の宗教的精神的な師でった。
== 交通アクセス == JR八幡大橋駅からバスで高石浜停留所下車、徒歩5分
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書 |author=[[田辺希文]]|editor=[[鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三]]|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{cite book|和書 |author=[[保田光則]]|title=[[新撰陸奥風土記]]|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{cite book|和書 |author=[[佐久間洞巖]]|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=新人物往来社|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ 早馬神社（公式ウェブサイト)]

==歴史== [[建保]]5年([[1217年]])、 [[鶴岡八幡宮]]の臨時[[別当]]であった[[梶原景実]]は、[[源頼朝]]公の死亡、これを追うかのような[[梶原]]一族の没落、又、[[和田氏]]、[[畠山氏]]が滅んで行くのを見て、世を憂い鎌倉を離れ、[[蝦夷]] [[千島]]を目指して下り、[[藤原高衡]] (本吉四郎高衡)ゆかりの地である[[気仙沼市]] [[唐桑町]]石浜にたどり着いた。家の脇に社を建て、[[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公の御影を安置し、一族の冥福を祈り[[梶原神社]]を建立した。 [[梶原景実]]の僧名は[[専光房良暹]]。[[梶原景時]]の兄で、[[源頼朝]]の宗教的精神的な師であった。 [[梶原景実|景実]]はその後、[[早馬神社]]を[[創建]]し、以来、連綿と[[梶原氏]]子孫が[[宮司]]を務めている。

'''梶原神社'''（かじわらじんじゃ）は、[[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜にある神社。御祭神は[[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公。鎌倉武将[[梶原景時]]の兄、梶原景実が創建。
==祭神== [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公の三柱を祀る。
==歴史== [[建保]]5年([[1217年]])、 [[鶴岡八幡宮]]の臨時[[別当]]であった[[梶原景実]]は、[[源頼朝]]公の死亡、これを追うかのような[[梶原]]一族の没落、又、[[和田氏]]、[[畠山氏]]が滅んで行くのを見て、世を憂い鎌倉を離れ、[[蝦夷]] [[千島]]を目指して下り、[[藤原高衡]] (本吉四郎高衡)ゆかりの地である[[気仙沼市]] [[唐桑町]]石浜にたどり着いた。家の脇に社を建て、[[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公の御影を安置し、一族の冥福を祈り[[梶原神社]]を建立した。 [[梶原景実]]の僧名は[[専光房良暹]]。[[梶原景時]]の兄で、[[源頼朝]]の宗教的精神的な師であった。 [[梶原景実|景実]]はその後、[[早馬神社]]を[[創建]]し、以来、連綿と[[梶原氏]]子孫が[[宮司]]を務めている。

'''梶原神社'''（かじわらじんじゃ）は、[[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜にある神社。御祭神は[[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公。景時の兄である梶原景実（[[専光房良暹]]）が創建した。
== 祭神 == [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公の三柱を祀る。
== 歴史 == [[建保]]5年([[1217年]])、 [[鶴岡八幡宮]]の臨時[[別当]]であった梶原景実（[[専光房良暹]]）は、[[源頼朝]]の死去、それを追うかのような梶原一族の没落、また[[和田氏]]、[[畠山氏]]が滅んで行くのを見て、世を憂い鎌倉を離れ、[[蝦夷]][[千島]]を目指して下り、[[藤原高衡]]（本吉四郎高衡）ゆかりの地である石浜（[[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]）にたどり着いた。家の脇に社を建て、源頼朝、[[梶原景時]]、[[梶原景季]]の御影を安置し、一族の冥福を祈り梶原神社を建立した。専光房は源頼朝の宗教的精神的な師であった。専光房はその後、[[早馬神社]]を創建し、以来、連綿と梶原氏直系子孫が[[宮司]]を務めている。
== 交通アクセス == JR[[八幡大橋駅]]からバスで高石浜停留所下車、徒歩5分

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |画像 = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |祭神 = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |社格 = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |画像 = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |祭神 = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |社格 = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |画像 = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |祭神 = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |社格 = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |画像 = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |祭神 = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |社格 = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |画像 = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |祭神 = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |社格 = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |画像 = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |祭神 = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |社格 = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.
== References ==

{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217年}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217th year }} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅}}.

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保|12=draft}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当|12=draft}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏|12=draft}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=draft}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=draft}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=draft}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=draft}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=draft|12=draft}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=draft|12=draft}}.

* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa|ja|唐桑町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|ja|気仙沼市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|ja|梶原景季|12=draft|12=draft}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|ja|1217th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kenpō|ja|建保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|ja|畠山氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Ezo|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=draft|12=draft}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=draft|12=draft}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{神社 |名称 = 梶原神社 |画像 = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |祭神 = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |社格 = |創建 = [[1217年]]（[[建保]]5年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''梶原神社'''（かじわらじんじゃ）は、[[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜にある神社。御祭神は[[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公。景時の兄である梶原景実（[[専光房良暹]]）が創建した。
== 祭神 == [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公の三柱を祀る。
== 歴史 == [[建保]]5年([[1217年]])、 [[鶴岡八幡宮]]の臨時[[別当]]であった梶原景実（[[専光房良暹]]）は、[[源頼朝]]の死去、それを追うかのような梶原一族の没落、また[[和田氏]]、[[畠山氏]]が滅んで行くのを見て、世を憂い鎌倉を離れ、[[蝦夷]][[千島]]を目指して下り、[[藤原高衡]]（本吉四郎高衡）ゆかりの地である石浜（[[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]）にたどり着いた。家の脇に社を建て、源頼朝、[[梶原景時]]、[[梶原景季]]の御影を安置し、一族の冥福を祈り梶原神社を建立した。専光房は源頼朝の宗教的精神的な師であった。専光房はその後、[[早馬神社]]を創建し、以来、連綿と梶原氏直系子孫が[[宮司]]を務めている。
== 交通アクセス == JR[[八幡大橋駅]]からバスで高石浜停留所下車、徒歩5分
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ 早馬神社（公式ウェブサイト)]

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}

{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[宮城県]][[気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅}}.

The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]

Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.

The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]

In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.

There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
