{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|qq=|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|qq=|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.

The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:梶原神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Karakuwa, Miyagi|Karakuwa]], [[:en:Kesennuma|Kesennuma]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. The enshrined deities are [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]] * [[:en:Kajiwara Kagesue|Kajiwara Kagesue]]
== History ==
In [[:en:1217|1217]] ([[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]] 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary [[:en:Bettō|Bettō]] of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]], witnessed the passing of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and [[:en:Hatakeyama clan|Hatakeyama clan]]. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the [[:en:Emishi|Ezo]] and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa, Miyagi|en|Karakuwa, Miyagi|WD=Q6368168|lt=Karakuwa}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|en|Kesennuma|WD=Q235746|lt=Kesennuma}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|en|Kajiwara Kagesue|WD=Q3192026|lt=Kajiwara Kagesue}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|en|Kajiwara Kagesue|WD=Q3192026|lt=Kajiwara Kagesue}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|en|1217|WD=Q5379|lt=1217}} ({{ill|Kempo (era)|en|Kempo (era)|WD=Q1194251|lt=Kenpō}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|WD=Q2900274|lt=Bettō}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|en|Hatakeyama clan|WD=Q662881|lt=Hatakeyama clan}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Ezo}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa, Miyagi|en|Karakuwa, Miyagi|WD=Q6368168|lt=Karakuwa}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|en|Kesennuma|WD=Q235746|lt=Kesennuma}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|en|Kajiwara Kagesue|WD=Q3192026|lt=Kajiwara Kagesue}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|en|Kajiwara Kagesue|WD=Q3192026|lt=Kajiwara Kagesue}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|en|1217|WD=Q5379|lt=1217}} ({{ill|Kempo (era)|en|Kempo (era)|WD=Q1194251|lt=Kenpō}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|WD=Q2900274|lt=Bettō}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|en|Hatakeyama clan|WD=Q662881|lt=Hatakeyama clan}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Ezo}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa, Miyagi|en|Karakuwa, Miyagi|WD=Q6368168|lt=Karakuwa|1=Karakuwa, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|en|Kesennuma|WD=Q235746|lt=Kesennuma|1=Kesennuma}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Kajiwara Kagetoki}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|en|Kajiwara Kagesue|WD=Q3192026|lt=Kajiwara Kagesue|1=Kajiwara Kagesue}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Kajiwara Kagetoki}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|en|Kajiwara Kagesue|WD=Q3192026|lt=Kajiwara Kagesue|1=Kajiwara Kagesue}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|en|1217|WD=Q5379|lt=1217|1=1217}} ({{ill|Kempo (era)|en|Kempo (era)|WD=Q1194251|lt=Kenpō|1=Kempo (era)}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|WD=Q2900274|lt=Bettō|1=Bettō}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418057}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|en|Hatakeyama clan|WD=Q662881|lt=Hatakeyama clan|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Ezo|1=Emishi}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |author=田辺希文|authorlink=田辺希文|editor=鈴木省三 (郷土史家)|鈴木省三|chapter=[[本吉郡]]|series=[[仙台叢書]]封内風土記 三|title=[[封内風土記]] 巻之十四|publisher=仙台叢書出版協会|ref={{Sfnref|田辺|1772}}|id={{NDLJP|763473}}|origdate=1772|date=1893|ncid=BN11172717}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=保田光則|title=新撰陸奥風土記|publisher=歴史図書社|origdate=1860|date=1980|id={{NDLJP|9570404}}|ncid=BN01896845}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=佐久間洞巖|authorlink=佐久間洞巖|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 巻之九 [[気仙郡]]|publisher=宮城県|id={{NDLJP|993129}}|date=1883|ncid=BA55683008}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=伊勢斉助|series=仙台叢書 第十六巻|title=奥羽観蹟聞老志 補修篇 巻之九 本吉郡|publisher=仙台叢書刊行会|oregdate=1923|date=1929|ncid=BN06896627}} * {{Cite book|和書 |author=梶原等|title=梶原景時：知られざる鎌倉本体の武士|publisher=[[新人物往来社]]|id=|date=2004|ISBN=4404031874}}
== External Links == * [http://hayama.jinja.jp/ Official website of Hayama Shrine]

{{No footnotes|date=2022-12}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = 梶原神社 |image = [[File:Kajiwara-jinja.jpg|thumb|梶原神社拝殿]] |location = [[:en:宮城県|宮城県]][[:en:気仙沼市|気仙沼市]][[唐桑町]]高石浜126 |位置 = |deity = [[:en:源頼朝|源頼朝]]公、[[梶原景時]]公、[[梶原景季]]公 |type = |創建 = [[1217]]（[[建保]]5th year ） |本殿 = |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }}
'''Kajiwara Shrine''' (梶原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Karakuwa, Miyagi|en|Karakuwa, Miyagi|qid=Q6368168|lt=Karakuwa|1=Karakuwa, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Kesennuma|en|Kesennuma|qid=Q235746|lt=Kesennuma|1=Kesennuma}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|qid=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Kajiwara Kagetoki}}, and {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|en|Kajiwara Kagesue|qid=Q3192026|lt=Kajiwara Kagesue|1=Kajiwara Kagesue}}. The shrine was founded by {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119924472}}, also known as Sengōbō Ryōzen (専光房良暹), Kagetoki's brother.
== Enshrined Deities ==
The shrine venerates the three deities: * {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|qid=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Kajiwara Kagetoki}} * {{ill|Kajiwara Kagesue|en|Kajiwara Kagesue|qid=Q3192026|lt=Kajiwara Kagesue|1=Kajiwara Kagesue}}
== History ==
In {{ill|1217|en|1217|qid=Q5379|lt=1217|1=1217}} ({{ill|Kempo (era)|en|Kempo (era)|qid=Q1194251|lt=Kenpō|1=Kempo (era)}} 5), {{ill|Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja|梶原景実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara Kagemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 専光房良暹|1=Senkōbō Ryōsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119924472}} (Sengōbō Ryōzen), who was the temporary {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|qid=Q2900274|lt=Bettō|1=Bettō}} of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qid=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}}, witnessed the passing of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, the subsequent decline of the Kajiwara family, and the fall of other notable clans such as the {{ill|Wada clan|ja|和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan|1=Wada clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11418057}} and {{ill|Hatakeyama clan|en|Hatakeyama clan|qid=Q662881|lt=Hatakeyama clan|1=Hatakeyama clan}}. Saddened by these events, he left Kamakura and journeyed northward, aiming for the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Ezo|1=Emishi}} and {{ill|Kuril Islands|ja|千島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuril Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千島列島|1=Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q80389}}.
Eventually, Kagemasa settled in Takai-shi-hama (currently in Karakuwa, Kesennuma City), a region associated with {{ill|Fujiwara Takahira|ja|藤原高衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Takahira|1=Takahira Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623654}} (Motoyoshi Shiro Takahira). There, he constructed a shrine beside his residence to house images of Yoritomo, Kagetoki, and Kagesue, praying for the repose of their spirits and establishing Kajiwara Shrine. Sengōbō was a spiritual mentor to Minamoto no Yoritomo. Later, he also founded {{ill|Hayama Shrine|ja|早馬神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Shrine|qq=}}, and the hereditary position of {{ill|guji|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guji|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453500}} (chief priest) was maintained by the direct descendants of the Kajiwara family.
== Access ==
The shrine is a 5-minute walk from the Takai-shi-hama bus stop, accessible via JR {{ill|Yahata Bridge Station|ja|八幡大橋駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Bridge Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡大橋（東陵高校）駅|1=Hachiman-Ōhashi（Tōryō High School) Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28688830}}.
== References ==
